title: Biolistic dye loading protocol for marine phytoplankton
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9va5b","text":"A method to load algal cells with dextran-conjugated fluorescent dyes. This is a good way of testing and optimising conditions for biolistic DNA delivery.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Weigh out 30 mg of 0.6 mm gold particles
Wash 3x with 1 ml 70% ethanol
Resuspend in 500 µl 70% ethanol (final concentration 60 mg/ml)
Mix gold thoroughly, take 60 µl into new tube
Spin 13000 rpm, 1 min
Remove supernatant
Wash with 200 µl deionised water - sonicate - centrifuge - remove supernatant
Add 8 µl of 8 mM FITC-dextran
Mix very thoroughly
Pipette 2 µl of mixture onto four carrier discs and allow to dry
Store at 4°C until use
Take 15 ml of exponentially growing algal culture (at approximately 5 x10 5 cells/ml). Healthy cells are essential.
Spin 3000 rpm, 3 min
Remove supernatant. Resuspend cells in 1 ml of loading buffer (10 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 1 M sorbitol, 200 mM potassium glutamate)
Spin 3000 rpm, 1 min
Remove supernatant using pipette
Add 50 µl of loading buffer, mix very gently
Pipette onto nitrocellulose filter (0.2 µm) positioned on the base of 35 mm Petri dish. Spread cells thinly using pipette tip. Allow excess liquid to soak into filter, but do not allow to dry.
Set up Bio-Rad PDS-1000 biolistic device. Insert rupture disk (1100 psi).
Place dish containing algal cells on second lowest shelf
FIRE!
Add 1ml of f/2 seawater media.
Spin 2000 rpm, 1 min
Remove supernatant (this is necessary to remove background dye fluorescence)
Resuspend cells in 1 ml of f/2 seawater media
Examine cells for dye fluorescence under fluorescent microscope.
creator: Glen Wheeler
